# gaps.md — corpus maintenance report (generated by the maintainer; a human rules on each item)
*Scan date: <date> · <N> nodes · <M> reading-entries. Nothing here is auto-fixed. Each item is a question for a ruling.*

## 1. Missing nodes (referenced, no page)
- [ ] `<name>` — referenced in <entry> ("<quote>") — create a card? (Y → runs through researcher→grader→carder)

## 2. Load-bearing thin cards (expansion candidates, ranked by how hard an entry leans)
- [ ] `<node>` — <entry> leans on it for "<claim>", card is <N> sentences — expand?

## 3. Edge/prose contradictions (frontmatter vs prose)
- [ ] `<node>` edge → `<target>`: frontmatter says "<type/standing>", prose says "<quote>". Which is right?

## 4. Cross-entry tensions (surface only — do NOT reconcile)
- [ ] <entryA> says "<quote>"; <entryB> says "<quote>". Keep the tension (mark seam) or resolve? Human ruling required.

## 5. Orphan nodes (zero backlinks)
- [ ] `<node>` — nothing references it. Add a link, or retire?

## 6. Standing drift (essay claims more than node grade supports)
- [ ] <entry> asserts "<claim>" but `<node>` grades it <standing>. Soften the essay, or upgrade the node's evidence?

## 7. Vocabulary leaks (missed by mechanical guards)
- [ ] `<location>`: "<leaked token>" — translate to plain language.

**Counts:** missing <n> · thin <n> · contradictions <n> · tensions <n> · orphans <n> · drift <n> · leaks <n>
